shots.vo: I'm Jeffrey Scudder — a painter who spent the last decade building instruments instead of paintings.
shots.prompt: Slow reverent cinematic push-in on the laptop; the screen glows brighter and the colored mark shimmers to life; dust motes drift.
The one signature morph: emblem laptop screen dissolves into the live notepat UI as the melody bed begins.

shots.vo: This is notepat — a software instrument. Keyboard, touch, MIDI, even pressure.
shots.prompt: Screen capture: notepat playing, tiles lighting as notes trigger, waveform reacting. Hard cuts between wide UI and macro on the tiles.

shots.vo: Under it, a 128-voice synthesizer I built from scratch — it models real instruments instead of playing back samples.
shots.prompt: Chrome number card: '128 PHYSICALLY-MODELED VOICES', number animates 1 to 128, over abstract waveguide/string-resonance motion. Tri-layer backlight.

shots.vo: The wild part — notepat runs anywhere. Your browser, your phone, and as the first thing that boots on a salvaged fifty-dollar laptop. No operating system underneath it.
shots.prompt: Three accelerating cuts: browser, phone, then the bare-metal boot beauty shot — warm screen-glow blooming. 'No OS underneath it' lands on the boot frame.

shots.vo: It grows out of Aesthetic Computer — where eighteen thousand people have already made sixteen thousand little programs.
shots.prompt: Machine-gun montage of KidLisp $code pieces, then a traction card '18,000 MAKERS · 16,000 PROGRAMS' chrome-stamps in.

shots.vo: My company turns that into instruments people play, and pay for. I want to make computing something you play again. That's what I'd do with Restless Egg.
shots.prompt: Emblem returns and holds; chrome title 'MAKE COMPUTING SOMETHING YOU PLAY AGAIN'; end card: notepat.com · aesthetic.computer · Aesthetic Inc. × Restless Egg.
